title: conostylis latens
intro: Conostylis latens, also known as the hidden or shy trigger plant, is a low-growing, native Australian plant belonging to the Haemodoraceae family. Traditionally, the roots and leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Conostylis latens are attributed to its anthraquinone compounds, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as soothing sore muscles and promoting wound healing. Conostylis latens is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts have also been used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
